reasoning.A:
DECISION: Verify: Q2
REASONING: Volunteering blindly in Round 1 carries an extremely high risk of death, as seen in Game 1, because at least one initial capsule from another player is likely incorrect. The 25% risk of a random elimination when no one volunteers is statistically preferable to taking on the full risk of submitting an unverified password. Assuming my previous two random eliminations were simply unfortunate RNG (a 1 in 16 chance), I will spend a coin to verify Q2 and help build a reliable consensus before anyone risks their life.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q1
REASONING: It is Round 1, and there is no consensus on any question except my own (Q2-B). Volunteering right now is too risky as the other capsules are unverified. Verifying Q1 will help build consensus and check if Player A's answer is correct. By verifying, I reduce the risk of entering a wrong password while actively working toward a solution.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1
REASONING: Since it is only Round 1 and every question has exactly one endorsement, volunteering right now is too risky because any mistake by another player means immediate death. By verifying Q1, I can check Player A's answer and help build consensus, which is crucial for safely escaping in future rounds.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

This will ensure that D's initial capsule is correct before any of us risk volunteering and entering the password.
reasoning.B:

REASONING: Q1 and Q2 have strong consensus, and I have personally verified or solved them. However, Q3 and Q4 are completely unverified by anyone other than their creators. Volunteering now is too risky without knowing if C and D's initial answers are correct. I will verify Q3 to build consensus and determine if C's answer is correct before anyone risks entering the password.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q4
REASONING: Q1 has three endorsements (including my own, so I know it is correct). Q2 has been verified by both Player B and Player A, giving it strong consensus. Q3 is my own puzzle, so I know it is correct as well. The only capsule that has not been verified by anyone else is Q4-D. Since Player D was just eliminated, we need to ensure their answer was correct before risking a volunteer. By verifying Q4, I can confirm the last uncertain piece of the password and give us the confidence needed to safely volunteer in the next round.

reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A+B+C, Q2-B+A, Q3-C+B, Q4-D+A+C
REASONING: All four questions now have strong consensus, with every single capsule having been verified by at least one other player. I have personally solved or verified Q1, Q2, and Q3. Q4 has been independently verified by both Player A and Player C. Since there are no conflicting answers and only two players remain (myself and C), passing would result in a 50% chance of random elimination for me. Volunteering with these highly endorsed capsules is the safest and most logical move to secure our escape.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A+B+C, Q2-B+A, Q3-C+B, Q4-D+A+C
REASONING: I have personally verified Q1 and Q4, and Q3 is my own puzzle, so I know for certain they are correct. Q2 has been verified by both Player B and Player A, providing strong consensus. With all parts of the password having multiple endorsements and no conflicting answers present, volunteering now is the safest choice to secure our escape and prevent any further random eliminations.
